info.name: climate service -> dataset
info.description: Send capabilities to the datasets available at climate service downstream.

info.name: datasets -> timeseries
info.description: Get timeseries capabilties from a dataset.

defaultConfig.no_of_locations_at_once_desc: number of locations to send at once
defaultConfig.continue_after_location_id_desc: continue after a particular location id
defaultConfig.create_substream_desc: create a substream for each datasets' timeseries
defaultConfig.maintain_incoming_substreams_desc: if false, ignore bracket IPs, thus flatten incoming substreams

info.name: timeseries capability -> data
info.description: Get the actual data from a timeseries capability.

info.name: timeseries data -> csv
info.description: Create CSV string out of timeseries data.

info.name: output to console
info.description: Output input to console.

info.name: africa calibration
info.description: Consumer to work in an Africa calibration flow.

info.name: ordered flatten nested dicts
info.description: ordered_flatten_nested_dicts

info.name: read csv
info.description: Read a csv file and send content as string downstream.

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: A single row from the CSV file sent as Setup struct.

id_col.desc: The column to be used to unique identify a row.

col_to_field_names.desc: Map CSV column names to field names in the Cap'n Proto struct. E.g. {'col1': 'field1', 'col2': 'field2'}.

send_ids.desc: Send only these ids as messages downstream. E.g. [1,2,3]

file.desc: The path to the CSV file to be read.

struct_type.desc: The Cap'n Proto struct type to fill from a CSV row.

to_attr.desc: Instead of sending a row as IP content, send it in this attribute.

info.name: read file
info.description: Read a file and send full string or lines downstream.

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: Output either full file content or each line as as separate message.

lines_mode.desc: Send single lines if true else send whole file content at once.

skip_lines.desc: If lines mode is true, skip that many lines at the beginning of the file.

info.name: write file
info.description: Write input into a file.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: The input data to be written to a file.

id_attr.desc: The attribute to get id for the filepattern from

from_attr.desc: Instead of the IP content, get the content from that 'attr'.

filepath_pattern.desc: The pattern to use for the filename. Can contain {id} as placeholder for the id attribute.

path_to_out_dir.desc: The path to the output directory where the files will be written.

append.desc: If True, append to existing files instead of overwriting them.

debug.desc: If True, print debug information to the console.

info.name: create lat lon coords
info.description: Create lat/lon coords for region.

inPorts.name: ids
inPorts.desc: List of IDs to include in the output.

inPorts.name: region
inPorts.desc: The region we create the coords for.

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: Either a stream of LatLonCoords or a serialized JSON array [[lat1,lon1],[lat2,lon2]] of lat/lon pairs.

stream.desc: If True, the component will stream the output Lat/Lon coord by Lat/Lon coord.

create_substream.desc: If true, creates for each set of region and ids, a new substream.

resolution.desc: Select the resolution of the generated data.

ids.desc: List of IDs to include in the output, unless the 'ids' port is connected.

path_to_ids_grid.desc: Path to the ids grid file.

info.name: get lat/lon grid value
info.description: Get value from a lat/lon grid.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: Lat/Lon coordinate to get the value at.

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: Value at the given lat/lon coordinate.

path_to_grid.desc: Path to the lat/lon grid file.

type.desc: Type of value to read from grid.

info.name: Proj transform coords
info.description: Transform coordinates using the Proj library.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: Input geo coordinate.

from_name.desc: Source CRS name: One of LatLon, WGS84, GKx (x=2-5), UTMab (a=[1-60], b=[C-X]).

to_name.desc: Target CRS name: One of LatLon, WGS84, GKx (x=2-5), UTMab (a=[1-60], b=[C-X]).

from_attr.desc: Attribute name to use as the input coordinate.

to_attr.desc: Attribute name to use as the output.

info.name: to geo coord
info.description: Create [geo_capnp:LatLonCoord | geo_capnp:UTMCoord | geo_capnp:GKCoord] from a list of values.

inPorts.name: vals
inPorts.desc: Values to convert into coord.

to_name.desc: One of 2D, XY, LatLon, WGS84, GKx (x=2-5), UTMab (a=[1-60], b=[C-X])

info.name: Use grid service
info.description: Use the grid service to get the grid value at a given Lat/Lon coord.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: The coordinate to get the value at.

inPorts.name: service
inPorts.desc: Capability or sturdy ref to service.

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: Output grid value at given coordinate.

as_common_value.desc: Send the output as a common.capnp:Value structure instead of grid.capnp:Grid.Value.

from_attr.desc: Attribute name to use as the input coordinate (a geo.capnp:LatLonCoord).

to_attr.desc: Attribute name to use as the output (a grid.capnp:Grid.Value or a common.capnp:Value).

calc.desc: If 'f(gv)' has a value, define an simple arithmetic expression named 'f(gv)', which can use 'gv' (grid value) and possible other variables defined in the 'calc' object.

info.name: add attribute
info.description: Add attribute to incoming IP.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: Arbitrary content.

inPorts.name: attr
inPorts.desc: Arbitrary content to store as attached attribute with name 'to_attr'.

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: IP (from in port) and attribute 'to_attr' containing content from attr port.

to_attr.desc: The attribute's name to add to the outgoing message.

info.name: copy
info.description: Copy IP to multiple outputs.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: The IP to copy to all attached outports

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: Copied IP for each attached outport

info.name: lift attributes
info.description: Lift attributes.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: Input message with any content containing some structured attributes.

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: The same content as the message on 'in', but with some attributes lifted out of a structured attribute into the top level attribute metadata.

lift_from_type.desc: Capnp struct type to read from attribute.

lifted_attrs.desc: Attributes to lift from struct into metadata.

info.name: Update JSON
info.description: Update JSON datastructures.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: JSON text which is supposed to be updated and changed.

types.desc: Define the loadable type the attribute being referenced has.

update.desc: List of update operations to perform on the JSON structure. Structure and description have to match.

replace.desc: List of replacement operations to perform on the JSON structure.

add.desc: List of addition operations to perform on the JSON structure.

info.name: ILR seed/harvest dates
info.description: Get closest ILR seed/harvest dates to lat/lon location.

info.name: Create model.capnp:Env with MONICA payload
info.description: Create model.capnp:Env with MONICA JSON env payload.

inPorts.name: climate
inPorts.desc: Climate data for MONICA simulation, either as a TimeSeries capability or a path to a CSV file.

inPorts.name: soil
inPorts.desc: Soil profile data for MONICA simulation, either as a Profile capability or a path to a JSON file containing an array of soil layers.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: MONICA env json.

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: An Env structure with possible attached climate/soil capabilities ready to be sent to a MONICA Cap'n Proto service or component.

from_attr.desc: Instead of the message content, read the MONICA JSON env from this attribute.

to_attr.desc: Instead of sending the ready prepared MONICA Cap'n Proto env via the message content, send it via this attribute.

climate_attr.desc: Either a capability to a time series (via @ out of attribute) or the path to a MONICA compatible climate CSV file from config value.

soil_attr.desc: Either a capability to a soil profile (via @ out of attribute) or a string (JSON array) containing a MONICA soil profile description from config value.

id_attr.desc: Id of current env via @ out of attribute or a UUID4 will be automatically generated.

info.name: Create MONICA JSON env
info.description: Create MONICA JSON environment.

info.name: Write MONICA CSV
info.description: Write a MONICA CSV file.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: Receive MONICA JSON result.

path_to_out_dir.desc: Use path_to_out_dir if no out_path_attr is available in metadata of IP.

out_path_attr.desc: If out_path_attr is available, don't use path_to_out_dir.

id_attr.desc: Name of attribute which contains id to use for file name pattern.

filepath_pattern.desc: Pattern with 'id' field (csv_{id}.csv)]. Write files name where id is replaced.

csv_delimiter.desc: Like ','. Use this string as delimiter for csv output.

info.name: africa calibration producer
info.description: Producer to work in an Africa calibration flow.

info.name: Use soil service
info.description: Use the soil service to get the soil profiles at a given Lat/Lon coord.

inPorts.name: latlon
inPorts.desc: Lat/Lon coordinate

inPorts.name: service
inPorts.desc: Capability or sturdy ref to service.

from_attr.desc: Get a [geo.capnp.LatLonCoord] from the attribute 'from_attr' received on 'in' message.

to_attr.desc: Stores the result, a [grid.capnp.Grid:Value], to attribute 'to_attr' on 'out' message.

mandatory.desc: Which soil attributes are needed in the result to be valid?

optional.desc: Which soil attributes are needed in the result to be valid?

only_raw_data.desc: Just return data which are physically available from the data source. If false, data can be generated from the raw data to allow more params to be available mandatory

info.name: create SpotPy calibration params
info.description: Creates/sets up parameters for Spotpy calibration.

outPorts.name: params
outPorts.desc: output spotpy calibration params as json list string

path_to_calibrate_csv.desc: path to csv file with parameters to calibrate

info.name: read observed values
info.description: Read the observed values for the calibration.

inPorts.name: country_ids
inPorts.desc: [1,2,3] :string of serialized json array containing country ids

outPorts.name: out
outPorts.desc: {country_id: {year: yield}} :string of json serialized mapping from country id to year to yield

crop.desc: crop to calibrate, e.g. maize | millet | sorghum

default_country_ids.desc: string of serialized json array containing country ids

info.name: Spotpy calibration component
info.description: The actual component in the center of the calibration flow.

inPorts.name: init_params
inPorts.desc: key/value pair description of the parameters to calibrate

inPorts.name: obs_values
inPorts.desc: list of observations

inPorts.name: sim_values
inPorts.desc: list of simulated values

outPorts.name: sampled_params
outPorts.desc: [name1: value1, name2: value2, ...] list of param_name -> sampled value pairs

info.name: split string
info.description: Splits a string along delimiter.

info.name: split string2
info.description: Splits a string along delimiter.

info.name: collect into list
info.description: Collects values (parsed out of a string) into a list.

inPorts.name: in
inPorts.desc: Stream of strings, whose values to collect into a single list.

cast_to.desc: Cast text elements to any of these types.

info.name: Collect into list
info.description: Collects values (parsed out of a string) into a list.

no_of_elements.desc: Number of elements to collect into array. no_of_elements <= 0 means collect all elements from upstream. This means the component waits for the 'in' port to close, before sending a message on out.

cast_to.desc: Cast received value on 'in' port to either text, float or int values.

info.name: to string
info.description: Outputs input structures as string (if possible).

struct_type.desc: A loadable Cap'n Proto schema and the contained struct to parse the 'in' content to.
